title: The Automatic Reply
text_zh: 我给老板发了一条消息，问他明天的会议几点开始。他回了一个字：「好。」我不知道他是说好还是在忙。我又问了一遍：「是上午九点还是十点？」他又回了：「好。」我开始觉得比较奇怪。我打电话给他，他没有接。半个小时以后，他打电话回来说：「不好意思，我手机掉水里了，现在只能按到一个键。」我问他：「那你刚才怎么回的消息？」他说：「我设了自动回复，不管别人说什么，都回'好'。」我笑了半天。这大概是最没有用的自动回复了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ gěi lǎobǎn fā le yì tiáo xiāoxi, wèn tā míngtiān de huìyì jǐ diǎn kāishǐ. Tā huí le yí gè zì:「Hǎo.」Wǒ bù zhīdào tā shì shuō hǎo háishì zài máng. Wǒ yòu wèn le yí biàn:「Shì shàngwǔ jiǔ diǎn háishì shí diǎn?」Tā yòu huí le:「Hǎo.」Wǒ kāishǐ juéde bǐjiào qíguài. Wǒ dǎ diànhuà gěi tā, tā méiyǒu jiē. Bàn gè xiǎoshí yǐhòu, tā dǎ diànhuà huílái shuō:「Bù hǎoyìsi, wǒ shǒujī diào shuǐ lǐ le, xiànzài zhǐ néng àn dào yí gè jiàn.」Wǒ wèn tā:「Nà nǐ gāngcái zěnme huí de xiāoxi?」Tā shuō:「Wǒ shè le zìdòng huífù, bùguǎn biéren shuō shénme, dōu huí 'hǎo'.」Wǒ xiào le bàntiān. Zhè dàgài shì zuì méiyǒu yòng de zìdòng huífù le.
text_en: I messaged my boss asking what time tomorrow's meeting starts. He replied with one word: 'OK.' I didn't know if he meant OK or was just busy. I asked again: 'Nine or ten in the morning?' He replied: 'OK.' I started feeling rather confused. I called him — no answer. Half an hour later he called back: 'Sorry, my phone fell in water. I can only press one button now.' I asked: 'Then how did you reply to messages?' He said: 'I set up auto-reply. No matter what anyone says, it replies OK.' I laughed for ages. That's probably the most useless auto-reply ever.

questions.q_en: Why did the boss only reply 'OK'?

options.text: 手机掉水里了，设了自动回复
options.pinyin: shǒujī diào shuǐ lǐ le, shè le zìdòng huífù
options.text_en: phone fell in water, set auto-reply

questions.q_zh: 自动回复不管别人说什么都回什么？
questions.q_en: What does the auto-reply say regardless of the message?

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得这个自动回复怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does 'I' think of this auto-reply?

options.pinyin: zuì méiyǒu yòng de zìdòng huífù
yīnggāi dàjiā dōu shè yí gè

title: The Cooking Competition
text_zh: 我和室友决定比赛做饭。规则很简单：每人做一个菜，让楼下的保安大叔来当裁判。我做了一个番茄炒蛋，味道还可以，但是鸡蛋炒得比较碎。室友做了一个酸辣土豆丝，看起来不错，但是他忘了放盐。保安大叔先吃了我的，点了点头。又吃了室友的，喝了一大口水。我以为自己赢了。但是保安大叔说：「两个都不怎么好吃。不过你们请我吃了饭，所以我两个都给满分。」室友不服气地说：「那到底谁赢了？」保安大叔笑着说：「我赢了——我不用做饭就吃了两个菜。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ hé shìyǒu juédìng bǐsài zuò fàn. Guīzé hěn jiǎndān: měi rén zuò yí gè cài, ràng lóu xià de bǎo'ān dàshū lái dāng cáipàn. Wǒ zuò le yí gè fānqié chǎo dàn, wèidào hái kěyǐ, dànshì jīdàn chǎo de bǐjiào suì. Shìyǒu zuò le yí gè suānlà tǔdòu sī, kàn qǐlái búcuò, dànshì tā wàng le fàng yán. Bǎo'ān dàshū xiān chī le wǒ de, diǎn le diǎn tóu. Yòu chī le shìyǒu de, hē le yí dà kǒu shuǐ. Wǒ yǐwéi zìjǐ yíng le. Dànshì bǎo'ān dàshū shuō:「Liǎng gè dōu bù zěnme hǎochī. Búguò nǐmen qǐng wǒ chī le fàn, suǒyǐ wǒ liǎng gè dōu gěi mǎn fēn.」Shìyǒu bù fúqì de shuō:「Nà dàodǐ shéi yíng le?」Bǎo'ān dàshū xiàozhe shuō:「Wǒ yíng le——wǒ bú yòng zuò fàn jiù chī le liǎng gè cài.」
text_en: My roommate and I decided to have a cooking competition. Simple rules: each makes one dish, and the security guard downstairs judges. I made scrambled eggs with tomato — tasted OK, but the eggs were rather crumbly. My roommate made hot and sour shredded potatoes — looked good, but he forgot the salt. The guard ate mine first and nodded. Then ate my roommate's and took a big gulp of water. I thought I'd won. But the guard said: 'Neither is that great. But you treated me to a meal, so I give both full marks.' My roommate said indignantly: 'Then who won?' The guard smiled: 'I won — I ate two dishes without cooking.'

questions.q_en: What was wrong with the roommate's dish?

options.pinyin: tǔdòu qiē de tài dà le

questions.q_zh: 保安大叔给了什么分数？
questions.q_en: What score did the guard give?

options.pinyin: wǒ mǎn fēn, shìyǒu líng fēn
options.text_en: full marks for me, zero for roommate

options.pinyin: liǎng gè dōu gěi le mǎn fēn
liǎng gè dōu gěi le líng fēn

questions.q_en: Who did the guard say won?

title: The Museum's Quiet Room
text_zh: 我们城市的博物馆最近做了一件有意思的事情——在二楼开了一个「安静房间」。这个房间里什么展品都没有，只有几把椅子和一扇大窗户。墙上写着：「这个房间是给需要安静的人准备的。」刚开始大家觉得很奇怪：博物馆为什么要有一个空房间？但是越来越多的人开始去那里坐一坐。有人在那里看窗外的天空，有人闭着眼睛休息，有人只是发呆。馆长说：「除了看展品以外，我们觉得人也需要一个什么都不做的地方。」那个房间比较小，但是它给了很多人一个在城市里停下来的机会。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen chéngshì de bówùguǎn zuìjìn zuò le yí jiàn yǒuyìsi de shìqing——zài èr lóu kāi le yí gè「ānjìng fángjiān」. Zhège fángjiān lǐ shénme zhǎnpǐn dōu méiyǒu, zhǐyǒu jǐ bǎ yǐzi hé yí shàn dà chuānghu. Qiáng shàng xiězhe:「Zhège fángjiān shì gěi xūyào ānjìng de rén zhǔnbèi de.」Gāng kāishǐ dàjiā juéde hěn qíguài: bówùguǎn wèishénme yào yǒu yí gè kōng fángjiān? Dànshì yuè lái yuè duō de rén kāishǐ qù nàlǐ zuò yí zuò. Yǒu rén zài nàlǐ kàn chuāng wài de tiānkōng, yǒu rén bìzhe yǎnjīng xiūxi, yǒu rén zhǐshì fādāi. Guǎnzhǎng shuō:「Chúle kàn zhǎnpǐn yǐwài, wǒmen juéde rén yě xūyào yí gè shénme dōu bú zuò de dìfang.」Nàge fángjiān bǐjiào xiǎo, dànshì tā gěi le hěn duō rén yí gè zài chéngshì lǐ tíng xiàlái de jīhuì.
text_en: Our city's museum recently did something interesting — opened a 'quiet room' on the second floor. There are no exhibits in it, just a few chairs and a large window. On the wall it says: 'This room is for people who need quiet.' At first everyone found it strange: why would a museum have an empty room? But more and more people started going to sit there. Some watch the sky through the window, some rest with eyes closed, some just space out. The director said: 'Besides viewing exhibits, we think people also need a place to do nothing.' That room is rather small, but it gave many people a chance to pause in the city.

questions.q_en: What's in the quiet room?

options.text: 几把椅子和一扇大窗户
options.pinyin: jǐ bǎ yǐzi hé yí shàn dà chuānghu
options.text_en: a few chairs and a large window

questions.q_en: What do people do there?

options.text_en: watch the sky, rest, space out

questions.q_zh: 馆长觉得人除了看展品还需要什么？
questions.q_en: What does the director think people need besides exhibits?

options.text: 一个什么都不做的地方
options.pinyin: yí gè shénme dōu bú zuò de dìfang

title: The Corner Bookstore
text_zh: 街角有一家很小的书店，门口的牌子上写着：「只卖旧书。」老板是个五十多岁的女人，她说每一本旧书都被人翻过，里面留着上一个读者的痕迹——有人在书里夹了一张照片，有人在旁边写了一句话。她觉得这比新书有意思。我在那里买过一本旧小说，里面夹着一张电影票。电影票上的日期是十五年前的。我一边看书一边想：十五年前看这本书的人，现在在做什么呢？那家书店比较暗，书架上的灯也不太亮，但是每次走进去，都觉得时间慢了下来。除了书以外，那里卖的是别人的故事和记忆。
text_pinyin: Jiējiǎo yǒu yì jiā hěn xiǎo de shūdiàn, ménkǒu de páizi shàng xiězhe:「Zhǐ mài jiù shū.」Lǎobǎn shì ge wǔshí duō suì de nǚrén, tā shuō měi yì běn jiù shū dōu bèi rén fān guò, lǐmiàn liúzhe shàng yí gè dúzhě de hénjì——yǒu rén zài shū lǐ jiā le yì zhāng zhàopiàn, yǒu rén zài pángbiān xiě le yí jù huà. Tā juéde zhè bǐ xīn shū yǒuyìsi. Wǒ zài nàlǐ mǎi guò yì běn jiù xiǎoshuō, lǐmiàn jiāzhe yì zhāng diànyǐng piào. Diànyǐng piào shàng de rìqī shì shíwǔ nián qián de. Wǒ yìbiān kàn shū yìbiān xiǎng: shíwǔ nián qián kàn zhè běn shū de rén, xiànzài zài zuò shénme ne? Nà jiā shūdiàn bǐjiào àn, shūjià shàng de dēng yě bú tài liàng, dànshì měi cì zǒu jìnqù, dōu juéde shíjiān màn le xiàlái. Chúle shū yǐwài, nàlǐ mài de shì biéren de gùshi hé jìyì.
text_en: On the street corner there's a tiny bookstore. The sign says: 'Only used books.' The owner is a woman in her fifties who says every used book has been touched by someone, leaving the previous reader's traces — someone tucked in a photo, someone wrote a note in the margin. She finds this more interesting than new books. I once bought a used novel there with a movie ticket inside. The date on the ticket was fifteen years ago. As I read, I wondered: what is the person who read this book fifteen years ago doing now? The store is rather dim, the shelf lights not very bright, but every time you walk in, time seems to slow down. Besides books, what that shop sells is other people's stories and memories.

questions.q_en: What does this bookstore only sell?
What was inside the used book 'I' bought?

questions.q_zh: 老板觉得这家店除了书以外卖的是什么？
questions.q_en: Besides books, what does the owner think the shop sells?

title: The Handwritten Sign
text_zh: 小区门口有一个卖水果的老太太。她不识字，但是她的水果旁边总是放着一个手写的牌子。那些字是她孙子帮她写的。每天早上孙子上学之前，会帮她把今天的水果价格写在一张纸板上。字写得歪歪扭扭的，但是很认真。有一天下雨了，纸板被淋湿了，字看不清了。老太太比较着急，因为她不知道价格怎么写。旁边一个买菜的阿姨说：「没关系，我帮你写。」她拿了一支笔，把价格重新写了一遍。老太太谢了又谢。晚上孙子放学回来，看到牌子上的字不是自己写的，问：「奶奶，今天谁帮你写的？」老太太说：「一个好心的人。」
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū ménkǒu yǒu yí gè mài shuǐguǒ de lǎo tàitai. Tā bú shì zì, dànshì tā de shuǐguǒ pángbiān zǒng shì fàngzhe yí gè shǒuxiě de páizi. Nàxiē zì shì tā sūnzi bāng tā xiě de. Měi tiān zǎoshang sūnzi shàng xué zhīqián, huì bāng tā bǎ jīntiān de shuǐguǒ jiàgé xiě zài yì zhāng zhǐbǎn shàng. Zì xiě de wāiwāiniǔniǔ de, dànshì hěn rènzhēn. Yǒu yì tiān xià yǔ le, zhǐbǎn bèi lín shī le, zì kàn bù qīng le. Lǎo tàitai bǐjiào zhāojí, yīnwèi tā bù zhīdào jiàgé zěnme xiě. Pángbiān yí gè mǎi cài de āyí shuō:「Méi guānxi, wǒ bāng nǐ xiě.」Tā ná le yì zhī bǐ, bǎ jiàgé chóngxīn xiě le yí biàn. Lǎo tàitai xiè le yòu xiè. Wǎnshang sūnzi fàng xué huílái, kàn dào páizi shàng de zì búshì zìjǐ xiě de, wèn:「Nǎinai, jīntiān shéi bāng nǐ xiě de?」Lǎo tàitai shuō:「Yí gè hǎoxīn de rén.」
text_en: At the complex entrance there's an old woman selling fruit. She can't read, but there's always a handwritten sign by her fruit. Her grandson writes it. Every morning before school, he writes today's prices on a piece of cardboard. The handwriting is crooked but earnest. One rainy day the cardboard got soaked and the writing became illegible. The old woman was rather worried since she didn't know how to write the prices. A woman buying vegetables nearby said: 'Don't worry, I'll write for you.' She took a pen and rewrote the prices. The old woman thanked her again and again. When her grandson came back from school that evening and saw the writing wasn't his, he asked: 'Grandma, who helped you write today?' She said: 'A kind person.'

questions.q_en: Who writes the sign?
What happened to the sign after it rained?

options.pinyin: bèi lín shī le, zì kàn bù qīng le

questions.q_zh: 谁帮老太太重新写了价格？
questions.q_en: Who rewrote the prices?

options.pinyin: yí gè mǎi cài de āyí

title: The Photograph on the Fridge
text_zh: 我家冰箱上贴着一张很旧的照片。照片里是我三岁的时候，坐在爸爸肩膀上，笑得很开心。妈妈说那天是我第一次看到大海。我现在看了无数次海了，但是那张照片里的笑容，我再也做不出来了。每次我打开冰箱拿东西的时候，都会看到那张照片。有时候我会想：长大是不是就是慢慢忘记怎么笑的过程？后来我把那张照片拿下来，放在了自己房间的桌子上。不是因为不想看，而是因为我想把它放在一个比较重要的地方。冰箱每天被打开很多次，但有些照片值得你停下来，好好看一看。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā bīngxiāng shàng tiēzhe yì zhāng hěn jiù de zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn lǐ shì wǒ sān suì de shíhou, zuò zài bàba jiānbǎng shàng, xiào de hěn kāixīn. Māma shuō nà tiān shì wǒ dì-yī cì kàn dào dàhǎi. Wǒ xiànzài kàn le wúshù cì hǎi le, dànshì nà zhāng zhàopiàn lǐ de xiàoróng, wǒ zài yě zuò bù chūlái le. Měi cì wǒ dǎkāi bīngxiāng ná dōngxi de shíhou, dōu huì kàn dào nà zhāng zhàopiàn. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì xiǎng: zhǎng dà shì bú shì jiùshì mànman wàngjì zěnme xiào de guòchéng? Hòulái wǒ bǎ nà zhāng zhàopiàn ná xiàlái, fàng zài le zìjǐ fángjiān de zhuōzi shàng. Búshì yīnwèi bù xiǎng kàn, ér shì yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng bǎ tā fàng zài yí gè bǐjiào zhòngyào de dìfang. Bīngxiāng měi tiān bèi dǎkāi hěn duō cì, dàn yǒuxiē zhàopiàn zhíde nǐ tíng xiàlái, hǎohǎo kàn yí kàn.
text_en: On our fridge there's a very old photo. In it I'm three years old, sitting on Dad's shoulders, smiling with pure happiness. Mom says that was the first time I saw the ocean. I've seen the ocean countless times now, but the smile in that photo — I can never make it again. Every time I open the fridge, I see that photo. Sometimes I wonder: is growing up just the slow process of forgetting how to smile? Later I took the photo down and put it on my bedroom desk. Not because I didn't want to see it, but because I wanted to put it somewhere more important. The fridge gets opened many times a day, but some photos deserve you stopping to take a good look.

questions.q_en: What is 'I' doing in the photo?

options.pinyin: zuò zài bàba jiānbǎng shàng xiào

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么把照片从冰箱上拿下来？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' take the photo off the fridge?

options.text: 想放在一个比较重要的地方
options.pinyin: xiǎng fàng zài yí gè bǐjiào zhòngyào de dìfang
options.text_en: wanted to put it somewhere more important

questions.q_en: What does 'I' think growing up might be?

options.text: 慢慢忘记怎么笑的过程
options.pinyin: mànman wàngjì zěnme xiào de guòchéng

title: The Lunch Box Note
text_zh: 我上大学的时候，妈妈每个星期会寄一个饭盒给我。饭盒里除了菜以外，总是有一张小纸条。纸条上写的都是很简单的话：「今天冷了，多穿一件。」「这个菜你小时候最爱吃。」「不要太晚睡。」我的室友们都很羡慕。有一次，纸条上写着：「妈妈把盐放多了，不好意思。」我尝了一口，确实比较咸。但是不知道为什么，那天的菜我觉得是最好吃的。因为那张纸条让我笑了。毕业以后，我把所有的纸条都收了起来，放在一个小盒子里。那些纸条被折得皱皱的，但是每一张上面都有妈妈的味道。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shàng dàxué de shíhou, māma měi gè xīngqī huì jì yí gè fànhé gěi wǒ. Fànhé lǐ chúle cài yǐwài, zǒng shì yǒu yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo. Zhǐtiáo shàng xiě de dōu shì hěn jiǎndān de huà:「Jīntiān lěng le, duō chuān yí jiàn.」「Zhège cài nǐ xiǎo shíhou zuì ài chī.」「Bú yào tài wǎn shuì.」Wǒ de shìyǒumen dōu hěn xiànmù. Yǒu yí cì, zhǐtiáo shàng xiězhe:「Māma bǎ yán fàng duō le, bù hǎoyìsi.」Wǒ cháng le yì kǒu, quèshí bǐjiào xián. Dànshì bù zhīdào wèishénme, nà tiān de cài wǒ juéde shì zuì hǎochī de. Yīnwèi nà zhāng zhǐtiáo ràng wǒ xiào le. Bìyè yǐhòu, wǒ bǎ suǒyǒu de zhǐtiáo dōu shōu le qǐlái, fàng zài yí gè xiǎo hézi lǐ. Nàxiē zhǐtiáo bèi zhé de zhòuzhòu de, dànshì měi yì zhāng shàngmiàn dōu yǒu māma de wèidào.
text_en: When I was in university, Mom sent me a lunch box every week. Besides food, there was always a little note inside. Simple things: 'It's cold today, wear an extra layer.' 'This dish was your childhood favorite.' 'Don't sleep too late.' My roommates were all envious. Once the note said: 'Mom added too much salt, sorry.' I took a bite — it was indeed rather salty. But for some reason, that day's food tasted the best to me. Because that note made me laugh. After graduating, I saved all the notes in a small box. They're creased from folding, but every one carries the scent of Mom.

questions.q_zh: 饭盒里除了菜还有什么？
questions.q_en: What was in the lunch box besides food?

questions.q_zh: 那天菜太咸了，「我」为什么觉得最好吃？
questions.q_en: The food was too salty — why did 'I' think it tasted best?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhǐtiáo ràng wǒ xiào le

questions.q_zh: 毕业以后「我」怎么处理那些纸条？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do with the notes after graduation?

options.text: 收起来放在一个小盒子里
options.pinyin: shōu qǐlái fàng zài yí gè xiǎo hézi lǐ
options.text_en: saved them in a small box

title: The Haircut
text_zh: 我留了三年的长头发，今天剪掉了。理发师问我：「确定吗？长头发很好看。」我说：「确定。」剪完以后，我看着镜子里的自己，觉得有点陌生。同事们的反应也不一样——有人说好看，有人说可惜了。妈妈看到照片以后说：「这样比较精神。」但是她的表情告诉我，她更喜欢长头发的我。我为什么要剪？因为我觉得那个留长头发的「我」已经不是现在的我了。人有时候需要改变一个外在的东西，来告诉自己：我跟以前不一样了。虽然只是头发，但是我把一些旧的东西也一起剪掉了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ liú le sān nián de cháng tóufa, jīntiān jiǎn diào le. Lǐfàshī wèn wǒ:「Quèdìng ma? Cháng tóufa hěn hǎokàn.」Wǒ shuō:「Quèdìng.」Jiǎn wán yǐhòu, wǒ kànzhe jìngzi lǐ de zìjǐ, juéde yǒudiǎn mòshēng. Tóngshìmen de fǎnyìng yě bù yíyàng——yǒu rén shuō hǎokàn, yǒu rén shuō kěxī le. Māma kàn dào zhàopiàn yǐhòu shuō:「Zhèyàng bǐjiào jīngshen.」Dànshì tā de biǎoqíng gàosu wǒ, tā gèng xǐhuan cháng tóufa de wǒ. Wǒ wèishénme yào jiǎn? Yīnwèi wǒ juéde nàge liú cháng tóufa de wǒ yǐjīng búshì xiànzài de wǒ le. Rén yǒu shíhou xūyào gǎibiàn yí gè wàizài de dōngxi, lái gàosu zìjǐ: wǒ gēn yǐqián bù yíyàng le. Suīrán zhǐshì tóufa, dànshì wǒ bǎ yìxiē jiù de dōngxi yě yìqǐ jiǎn diào le.
text_en: I cut my long hair today, after growing it for three years. The stylist asked: 'Are you sure? Long hair looks great.' I said: 'Sure.' After the cut, I looked at myself in the mirror and felt a little unfamiliar. Colleagues reacted differently — some said it looked good, some said it was a shame. Mom saw the photo and said: 'This looks more energetic.' But her expression told me she preferred the long-haired me. Why did I cut it? Because the long-haired 'me' was no longer who I am now. Sometimes people need to change something external to tell themselves: I'm different from before. It was just hair, but I cut away some old things along with it.

questions.q_en: How long had 'I' grown long hair?

questions.q_zh: 妈妈的真实想法是什么？
questions.q_en: What was Mom's real feeling?
Why did 'I' cut the hair?

options.text: 觉得长头发的自己已经不是现在的自己了
options.pinyin: juéde cháng tóufa de zìjǐ yǐjīng búshì xiànzài de zìjǐ le
options.text_en: the long-haired self isn't who I am now

title: The Night Music
text_zh: 最近每天晚上十点左右，我都能听到一阵很轻的钢琴声。声音从楼上传下来，弹的都是很慢很安静的曲子。我住的是老楼，隔音比较差，但是奇怪的是，其他邻居都说没听到。我开始怀疑是不是自己的问题。有一天我决定上去看看。我住五楼，六楼只有一户人家，是一个刚搬来的年轻人。我敲了门，他开了。我问他是不是在弹琴。他说：「是的，但是我用了耳机，你应该听不到才对。」我说：「但是我确实听到了。」他把我带到他的琴旁边——原来他的耳机线没有完全插进去，声音一直从钢琴的音箱里出来。他比较不好意思，但是我说：「没关系，我觉得比较好听。」
text_pinyin: Zuìjìn měi tiān wǎnshang shí diǎn zuǒyòu, wǒ dōu néng tīng dào yí zhèn hěn qīng de gāngqín shēng. Shēngyīn cóng lóu shàng chuán xiàlái, tán de dōu shì hěn màn hěn ānjìng de qǔzi. Wǒ zhù de shì lǎo lóu, géyīn bǐjiào chà, dànshì qíguài de shì, qítā línjū dōu shuō méi tīng dào. Wǒ kāishǐ huáiyí shì bú shì zìjǐ de wèntí. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ juédìng shàngqu kànkan. Wǒ zhù wǔ lóu, liù lóu zhǐyǒu yì hù rénjiā, shì yí gè gāng bān lái de niánqīng rén. Wǒ qiāo le mén, tā kāi le. Wǒ wèn tā shì bú shì zài tán qín. Tā shuō:「Shì de, dànshì wǒ yòng le ěrjī, nǐ yīnggāi tīng bú dào cái duì.」Wǒ shuō:「Dànshì wǒ quèshí tīng dào le.」Tā bǎ wǒ dài dào tā de qín pángbiān——yuánlái tā de ěrjī xiàn méiyǒu wánquán chā jìnqù, shēngyīn yìzhí cóng gāngqín de yīnxiāng lǐ chūlái. Tā bǐjiào bù hǎoyìsi, dànshì wǒ shuō:「Méi guānxi, wǒ juéde bǐjiào hǎo tīng.」
text_en: Recently around ten every night, I hear faint piano music. It drifts down from upstairs — always slow, quiet pieces. My building is old with rather poor soundproofing, but strangely, other neighbors say they don't hear it. I started wondering if it was just me. One day I decided to go check. I live on the fifth floor; the sixth has only one household — a young person who just moved in. I knocked and he answered. I asked if he was playing piano. He said: 'Yes, but I'm using headphones — you shouldn't be able to hear it.' I said: 'But I definitely can.' He took me to his piano — turns out his headphone jack wasn't fully plugged in, so sound had been coming through the piano's speakers all along. He was rather embarrassed, but I said: 'It's fine, I think it sounds rather nice.'

questions.q_en: What was the piano music like?

options.pinyin: hěn qīng hěn màn hěn ānjìng

questions.q_zh: 为什么其他邻居没听到？
questions.q_en: Why didn't other neighbors hear it?

options.text: 因为声音很轻，只有楼下的「我」能听到
options.pinyin: yīnwèi shēngyīn hěn qīng, zhǐyǒu lóu xià de wǒ néng tīng dào
options.text_en: sound was faint, only the person directly below could hear

questions.q_en: How was the sound leaking?

options.text: 耳机线没有完全插进去
options.pinyin: ěrjī xiàn méiyǒu wánquán chā jìnqù
options.text_en: headphone jack wasn't fully plugged in

title: The Wishing Tree
text_zh: 公园里有一棵很老的树。不知道从什么时候开始，人们在树枝上挂了很多小纸条，上面写着自己的愿望。我有时候会去看那些纸条。大部分写的是：「希望考试通过」「希望爸妈身体健康」「希望找到好工作」。有一张纸条比较特别，上面写着：「希望明天是晴天，因为我想去放风筝。」我笑了。那些大的愿望和小的愿望被风吹得一起飘着，好像都一样重要。有一天我也挂了一张。我写的是：「希望二十年以后，再来看这棵树的时候，我还能记得今天的自己。」那张纸条在风里轻轻动了一下，好像树点了点头。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yì kē hěn lǎo de shù. Bù zhīdào cóng shénme shíhou kāishǐ, rénmen zài shùzhī shàng guà le hěn duō xiǎo zhǐtiáo, shàngmiàn xiězhe zìjǐ de yuànwàng. Wǒ yǒu shíhou huì qù kàn nàxiē zhǐtiáo. Dàbùfen xiě de shì:「Xīwàng kǎoshì tōngguò」「xīwàng bà mā shēntǐ jiànkāng」「xīwàng zhǎo dào hǎo gōngzuò」. Yǒu yì zhāng zhǐtiáo bǐjiào tèbié, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Xīwàng míngtiān shì qíngtiān, yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng qù fàng fēngzhēng.」Wǒ xiào le. Nàxiē dà de yuànwàng hé xiǎo de yuànwàng bèi fēng chuī de yìqǐ piāozhe, hǎoxiàng dōu yíyàng zhòngyào. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ yě guà le yì zhāng. Wǒ xiě de shì:「Xīwàng èrshí nián yǐhòu, zài lái kàn zhè kē shù de shíhou, wǒ hái néng jìde jīntiān de zìjǐ.」Nà zhāng zhǐtiáo zài fēng lǐ qīngqīng dòng le yíxià, hǎoxiàng shù diǎn le diǎn tóu.
text_en: In the park there's a very old tree. At some point people started hanging small notes on its branches with their wishes. Sometimes I go read them. Most say: 'Hope I pass the exam,' 'Hope Mom and Dad stay healthy,' 'Hope I find a good job.' One note was rather special: 'Hope tomorrow is sunny, because I want to fly a kite.' I smiled. Big wishes and small wishes flutter together in the wind, seeming equally important. One day I hung one too. I wrote: 'I hope twenty years from now, when I come back to see this tree, I'll still remember today's me.' The note swayed gently in the wind, as if the tree nodded.

questions.q_zh: 那张比较特别的纸条写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the special note say?

options.text: 希望明天是晴天，想去放风筝
options.pinyin: xīwàng míngtiān shì qíngtiān, xiǎng qù fàng fēngzhēng
options.text_en: hope for sunny weather to fly a kite
hope to find a good job

questions.q_en: What wish did 'I' write?

options.text: 希望二十年后还能记得今天的自己
options.pinyin: xīwàng èrshí nián hòu hái néng jìde jīntiān de zìjǐ
options.text_en: remember today's self in twenty years
hope to be happy every day

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得大的愿望和小的愿望有什么关系？
questions.q_en: What does 'I' think about big and small wishes?
